pr.title: align object files in makefile log
pr.author: EpochFlame

summary: Tiny build-system cleanup: the Makefile compile log line for C object builds was changed from `@echo Compiling $<` to `@echo "Compiling " $<`, apparently to improve alignment/readability of object-file compilation messages in build output. No functional compile rule changes were made.
what_changed: Updated the C source object build rule in `Makefile`.
Changed the compile status message from `@echo Compiling $<` to `@echo "Compiling " $<`.
The assembler rule, dependency-generation command, and actual compiler invocation were unchanged.
smart_moves: Kept the change narrowly scoped to the human-facing build log output.
Avoided altering compiler flags, dependency generation, include paths, or object build semantics.
Used quoting around the literal `Compiling ` text, preserving the intended trailing spacing before the source filename in the echoed output.
decomp_lessons: Small Makefile UX improvements can make large decompilation build logs easier to scan without risking matching behavior.
When changing build output formatting, isolate the change from actual compiler, assembler, and dependency-generation commands.
For decomp workflows, build-system edits should distinguish clearly between cosmetic log changes and changes that could affect object output.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or object-matching tactic is evidenced; this PR only adjusted Makefile log formatting.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review body text were present in the provided PR slice.
searchable_terms: align object files in makefile log
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further adjust Makefile compile log formatting or quiet build output.
Search for Makefile rules using `@echo Compiling` to confirm consistency across C and assembly build messages.
Check whether later build-system changes standardized quoting or spacing in status messages.
